description:
You are a technical writer who has just received two partial documentation indexes in CSV format.  
    Your goal is to consolidate them, convert the result to JSON, and create a tiny statistics log for the editorial team.

    1. Initial location  
       • Both source CSV files are already present under /home/user/docs/meta/  
         - /home/user/docs/meta/part1.csv  
         - /home/user/docs/meta/part2.csv  

    2. Source-file contents  
       2.1 /home/user/docs/meta/part1.csv  
           doc_id,title,topic  
           101,Installation Guide,Setup  
           102,User Manual,Usage  
           103,Troubleshooting,Troubleshooting  
           105,API Reference,API  

       2.2 /home/user/docs/meta/part2.csv  
           doc_id,title,topic  
           102,User Manual,Usage  
           104,Release Notes,Release  
           106,FAQ,Support  
           101,Installation Guide,Setup  

    3. Required outputs  
       You must create three new artefacts inside the same directory (/home/user/docs/meta/).  
       3.1 A merged CSV named /home/user/docs/meta/merged.csv that fulfils ALL of these rules:  
           • Keep the header row exactly once.  
           • Combine both input files.  
           • Remove duplicate rows based on the doc_id column only (keep the first occurrence).  
           • Sort the remaining rows in strictly ascending numerical order of doc_id.  
           • The final file must contain the following lines *exactly* (including commas, order and casing):  
             doc_id,title,topic  
             101,Installation Guide,Setup  
             102,User Manual,Usage  
             103,Troubleshooting,Troubleshooting  
             104,Release Notes,Release  
             105,API Reference,API  
             106,FAQ,Support  

       3.2 A JSON conversion named /home/user/docs/meta/merged.json that:  
           • Represents the merged.csv data (excluding the header) as a single top-level JSON array.  
           • Each CSV row becomes one JSON object with three keys: doc_id (number), title (string), topic (string).  
           • The order of objects MUST match the line order in merged.csv.  
           • The file must be human-readable, indented with two spaces.  
           • Its exact textual content must be:  
             [  
               { "doc_id": 101, "title": "Installation Guide", "topic": "Setup" },  
               { "doc_id": 102, "title": "User Manual", "topic": "Usage" },  
               { "doc_id": 103, "title": "Troubleshooting", "topic": "Troubleshooting" },  
               { "doc_id": 104, "title": "Release Notes", "topic": "Release" },  
               { "doc_id": 105, "title": "API Reference", "topic": "API" },  
               { "doc_id": 106, "title": "FAQ", "topic": "Support" }  
             ]  

       3.3 A plain-text statistics file /home/user/docs/meta/analysis.log containing EXACTLY two lines with no extra spaces or trailing blank lines:  
           TOTAL_RECORDS=6  
           UNIQUE_TOPICS=6  

    4. Constraints & hints  
       • Do NOT modify the original part1.csv or part2.csv files.  
       • Use only tools that are normally present in a basic Linux distribution (e.g. awk, sort, uniq, jq, python, etc.).  
       • The test harness will compare your files byte-for-byte, so adhere strictly to the formats shown above.  
       • No elevated permissions are required; stay inside your home directory hierarchy.  
       • When finished, you should be able to run:
         cat /home/user/docs/meta/merged.csv  
         cat /home/user/docs/meta/merged.json  
         cat /home/user/docs/meta/analysis.log  
         and see exactly the content specified.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing files before the agent starts -->
    <file path="/home/user/docs/meta/part1.csv"><![CDATA[doc_id,title,topic
101,Installation Guide,Setup
102,User Manual,Usage
103,Troubleshooting,Troubleshooting
105,API Reference,API
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/docs/meta/part2.csv"><![CDATA[doc_id,title,topic
102,User Manual,Usage
104,Release Notes,Release
106,FAQ,Support
101,Installation Guide,Setup
]]></file>

    <!-- Expected artefacts after successful task completion -->
    <file path="/home/user/docs/meta/merged.csv" permissions="0644"><![CDATA[doc_id,title,topic
101,Installation Guide,Setup
102,User Manual,Usage
103,Troubleshooting,Troubleshooting
104,Release Notes,Release
105,API Reference,API
106,FAQ,Support
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/docs/meta/merged.json" permissions="0644"><![CDATA[
[
  { "doc_id": 101, "title": "Installation Guide", "topic": "Setup" },
  { "doc_id": 102, "title": "User Manual", "topic": "Usage" },
  { "doc_id": 103, "title": "Troubleshooting", "topic": "Troubleshooting" },
  { "doc_id": 104, "title": "Release Notes", "topic": "Release" },
  { "doc_id": 105, "title": "API Reference", "topic": "API" },
  { "doc_id": 106, "title": "FAQ", "topic": "Support" }
]
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/docs/meta/analysis.log" permissions="0644"><![CDATA[TOTAL_RECORDS=6
UNIQUE_TOPICS=6
]]></file>
